results.rendered_html: ka-stambhī f. (fr. ka, ‘head’ ? + stambha) the prop of a carriage-pole, piece of wood fastened on at the extreme end of the pole serving as a prop or rest (popularly called ‘sipoy’ in Western India, and in English, ‘horse’),  ŚBr. i, 1, 2, 9.
